Title: Order a multi-analyte lab panel and verify all member tests are generated
Preconditions: Clinician logged in with active visit; CBC panel configured as orderable
Test_Steps: 1. Open patient with active visit | 2. Start lab order entry | 3. Search and select "Complete Blood Count" panel | 4. Submit order | 5. Open the order details
Expected_Result: Single panel order creates child orders for every configured member analyte, each linked to the parent panel order

Title: Panel order with one disabled member excludes only that analyte
Preconditions: Metabolic panel configured with one member retired
Test_Steps: 1. Retire the Glucose member of BMP in concept dictionary | 2. Order BMP panel | 3. Inspect generated child orders
Expected_Result: Child orders generated for all active members; retired Glucose is excluded; no error raised

Title: Cancelling a parent panel order cascades to member orders
Preconditions: CBC panel order placed with member child orders active
Test_Steps: 1. Open active CBC panel order | 2. Discontinue the parent panel order | 3. Inspect each member order status
Expected_Result: All member child orders transition to DISCONTINUED; audit reflects cascade with timestamp and provider

Title: Order panel with no configured members is rejected
Preconditions: An empty panel concept exists with no set members
Test_Steps: 1. Order the empty panel | 2. Attempt to submit
Expected_Result: Order is rejected or warns that the panel has no orderable members; no orphan order persisted

Title: Collect specimen and record collection datetime, type and collector
Test_Steps: 1. Open lab worklist | 2. Select pending order | 3. Record specimen type, collection datetime and collector | 4. Mark as collected
Expected_Result: Order transitions to COLLECTED; specimen metadata persisted; collection event is auditable

Title: Reject specimen with documented rejection reason
Test_Steps: 1. Open collected specimen | 2. Choose Reject | 3. Select rejection reason "Hemolyzed" | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Specimen status set to REJECTED; rejection reason persisted; re-collection task auto-generated; results blocked

Title: Specimen rejection without a reason is blocked
Test_Steps: 1. Open collected specimen | 2. Choose Reject | 3. Leave reason blank | 4. Confirm
Expected_Result: Rejection is blocked with validation error requiring a rejection reason; status unchanged

Title: Pairwise specimen type vs container vs additive validation
Preconditions: Specimen type and container rules configured
Test_Steps: 1. Select specimen type | 2. Select container/additive | 3. Validate compatibility
Expected_Result: Compatible combinations are accepted; incompatible additive combinations raise a validation warning

Title: Collection datetime in the future is rejected
Test_Steps: 1. Open pending order | 2. Set collection datetime to tomorrow | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects future collection datetime with validation error

Title: Collection datetime before order datetime is rejected
Test_Steps: 1. Open order | 2. Set collection datetime 1 minute before order datetime | 3. Save
Expected_Result: System rejects collection earlier than order placement; clear validation message

Title: Generate unique accession number on specimen receipt
Preconditions: Specimen collected and received at lab
Test_Steps: 1. Receive specimen in lab module | 2. Trigger accession | 3. Inspect accession identifier
Expected_Result: A unique, non-reusable accession number is assigned and bound to the specimen and order

Title: Accession numbers are never duplicated under concurrent receipt
Test_Steps: 1. Receive two specimens concurrently in two sessions | 2. Compare assigned accession numbers
Expected_Result: Each specimen receives a distinct accession; no duplicate or collision; DB uniqueness constraint enforced

Title: Re-accession of a rejected-and-recollected specimen yields new accession
Test_Steps: 1. Recollect rejected specimen | 2. Receive and accession
Expected_Result: A new accession number is generated; original accession remains marked rejected and is not reused

Title: Enter numeric result for an analyte with valid units
Preconditions: Accessioned specimen with analyte ready for result
Test_Steps: 1. Open result entry for analyte | 2. Enter numeric value and unit | 3. Save as preliminary
Expected_Result: Result saved in PRELIMINARY state; value and unit persisted; awaiting verification

Title: Reject non-numeric value for a numeric analyte
Test_Steps: 1. Open result entry | 2. Enter alphabetic value | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Validation error: numeric value required; result not saved

Title: Boundary value at analyte allowable absolute min and max
Test_Steps: 1. Enter value at lower bound | 2. Enter value at upper bound | 3. Enter value above upper bound
Expected_Result: Min and max accepted; value above absolute max is rejected or flagged as implausible

Title: Enter coded qualitative result from allowed value set
Preconditions: Qualitative analyte with coded answers configured
Test_Steps: 1. Open result entry for coded analyte | 2. Select coded answer | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Only configured coded answers selectable; free text not allowed; selected code persisted

Title: Verify preliminary result to FINAL by authorized technologist
Preconditions: Preliminary result entered; verifier has Lab Verify privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open preliminary result | 2. Review value | 3. Click Verify
Expected_Result: Result transitions PRELIMINARY to FINAL; verifier and verification datetime recorded; result becomes visible to clinicians

Title: User without verify privilege cannot finalize results
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as entry-only user | 2. Open preliminary result | 3. Attempt to Verify
Expected_Result: Verify action is hidden or denied with authorization error; result stays PRELIMINARY

Title: Same user cannot enter and verify when two-person rule enforced
Test_Steps: 1. Enter result as TECH01 | 2. Attempt to verify same result as TECH01
Expected_Result: Self-verification blocked; system requires a different verifier; audit captures attempt

Title: Amend a finalized result and preserve original value
Preconditions: Finalized result exists; amender has privilege
Test_Steps: 1. Open final result | 2. Choose Amend | 3. Enter corrected value and reason | 4. Save
Expected_Result: Result transitions to AMENDED; original value retained in history; amendment reason, user and datetime audited
Healthcare_Classification: Patient Safety; Clinical; Audit; Data Integrity

Title: Amendment without reason is blocked
Test_Steps: 1. Open final result | 2. Choose Amend | 3. Change value | 4. Leave reason blank | 5. Save
Expected_Result: Amendment blocked with validation requiring a reason; original unchanged

Title: Amended result triggers re-notification to ordering provider
Preconditions: Amended result on a result previously communicated
Test_Steps: 1. Amend a finalized result | 2. Inspect provider notification queue
Expected_Result: An updated-result notification is generated to the ordering provider referencing the amendment

Title: Apply age-specific reference range to result interpretation
Preconditions: Analyte with pediatric and adult ranges configured
Test_Steps: 1. Enter result for a 5-year-old | 2. Enter same value for a 40-year-old | 3. Compare flags
Test_Data: Analyte=ALP; Value=300; Child range=high; Adult range=high-normal
Expected_Result: Interpretation flag derived from patient age band; pediatric flagged High, adult flagged Normal as configured

Title: Apply sex-specific reference range
Test_Steps: 1. Enter Hemoglobin for male patient | 2. Enter same value for female patient
Test_Data: Analyte=HGB; Value=13.0; Male low-normal; Female normal
Expected_Result: Flag computed using patient sex; male and female interpretations differ per configured ranges

Title: Result exactly at reference range boundary is flagged Normal
Test_Steps: 1. Enter value at lower bound | 2. Enter value at upper bound | 3. Enter value just below lower bound
Expected_Result: 3.5 and 5.1 flagged Normal; 3.4 flagged Low; boundary inclusive per configuration

Title: Missing reference range for analyte degrades gracefully
Test_Steps: 1. Enter result for analyte without range | 2. View interpretation
Expected_Result: Result saved with no interpretive flag and a clear "no reference range" indicator; no crash

Title: Delta check flags excessive change from prior result
Preconditions: Prior result exists; delta threshold configured
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve prior Creatinine result | 2. Enter new result exceeding delta threshold | 3. Observe flag
Expected_Result: Delta check triggers; result flagged for review and verification is gated pending confirmation

Title: No delta flag when change is within threshold
Preconditions: Prior result and delta threshold configured
Test_Steps: 1. Retrieve prior result | 2. Enter new result within threshold | 3. Observe absence of flag
Expected_Result: No delta flag raised; result proceeds normally to verification

Title: Delta check skipped when no prior result exists
Preconditions: Patient has no prior result for analyte
Test_Steps: 1. Enter first-ever result for analyte | 2. Observe delta evaluation
Expected_Result: Delta check is bypassed gracefully; result saved without delta flag; no error

Title: Delta check respects elapsed-time window
Preconditions: Delta valid only within configured time window
Test_Steps: 1. Enter result with prior outside window | 2. Enter result with prior inside window
Expected_Result: Delta evaluated only against prior within the time window; out-of-window prior ignored

Title: Critical result triggers mandatory callback workflow
Test_Steps: 1. Enter result in critical range | 2. Verify result | 3. Observe callback task
Expected_Result: Critical flag set; mandatory callback task created; result not considered closed until callback documented

Title: Document callback read-back acknowledgement
Preconditions: Critical result with open callback task
Test_Steps: 1. Open callback task | 2. Record recipient name, datetime and read-back confirmation | 3. Save
Expected_Result: Callback documented with read-back; task closes; full audit trail captured per CLIA/Joint Commission expectations

Title: Unacknowledged critical callback escalates after SLA
Test_Steps: 1. Generate critical result | 2. Leave callback unacknowledged past SLA | 3. Observe escalation
Expected_Result: Callback escalates to supervisor/backup contact after SLA breach; escalation logged

Title: Critical result cannot be auto-released without callback
Preconditions: Auto-release rule enabled for normal results only
Test_Steps: 1. Enter and verify a critical result | 2. Check auto-release behavior
Expected_Result: Auto-release is suppressed for critical results; manual callback required before release

Title: Lab order carries correct LOINC code for the analyte
Preconditions: Analyte mapped to LOINC in concept dictionary
Test_Steps: 1. Order analyte | 2. Inspect order code mappings | 3. Verify LOINC present
Expected_Result: Order/observation carries LOINC 2951-2 with correct system URI http://loinc.org

Title: Result Observation exposes LOINC and UCUM unit via FHIR
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; verified result exists
Test_Steps: 1. GET Observation for the result | 2. Inspect code.coding and valueQuantity
Expected_Result: Observation.code uses LOINC system; valueQuantity.code is valid UCUM; value and unit match entered result

Title: Analyte missing LOINC mapping is detectable
Test_Steps: 1. Order/result unmapped analyte | 2. Export to FHIR | 3. Inspect coding
Expected_Result: Observation falls back to local code only; absence of LOINC is detectable for data-quality reporting

Title: Pairwise LOINC component vs method vs system mapping
Preconditions: Multiple LOINC variants for similar analytes
Test_Steps: 1. Map analytes varying by component/method/system | 2. Validate distinct LOINC selected
Test_Data: Glucose-fasting vs random; Serum vs plasma
Expected_Result: Each analyte variant resolves to the correct distinct LOINC reflecting component, method and specimen

Title: Compute turnaround time from collection to verification
Test_Steps: 1. Collect specimen | 2. Accession | 3. Verify result | 4. View TAT metric
Expected_Result: TAT computed as collection-to-verification interval (2h30m) and shown on the order/result

Title: STAT order TAT measured against STAT SLA
Test_Steps: 1. Place STAT order | 2. Complete lifecycle | 3. Compare TAT to STAT SLA | 4. Repeat for Routine
Expected_Result: TAT compared against priority-specific SLA; breaches flagged; STAT held to tighter SLA

Title: TAT excludes time in REJECTED/recollection state per policy
Test_Steps: 1. Reject specimen | 2. Recollect | 3. Complete result | 4. Inspect TAT calculation
Expected_Result: TAT calculation accounts for rejection/recollection per configured policy; metric is defensible

Title: Reflex test auto-ordered when trigger condition met
Preconditions: Reflex rule: TSH abnormal triggers Free T4
Test_Steps: 1. Enter abnormal TSH result | 2. Verify result | 3. Observe reflex order
Expected_Result: Free T4 is automatically ordered as a reflex; reflex linkage recorded to triggering result

Title: No reflex when trigger condition not met
Test_Steps: 1. Enter normal TSH result | 2. Verify | 3. Confirm no reflex
Expected_Result: No reflex test ordered; only the original analyte present

Title: Reflex cascade does not loop infinitely
Test_Steps: 1. Trigger reflex that could re-trigger itself | 2. Observe cascade termination
Expected_Result: Reflex chain terminates safely; no infinite loop or duplicate runaway orders; guard enforced

Title: Pairwise reflex trigger by result value and patient attributes
Preconditions: Reflex rules vary by value and patient age/sex
Test_Steps: 1. Vary triggering value and patient demographics | 2. Validate reflex decision
Test_Data: Value high/low x Age adult/child x Sex M/F
Expected_Result: Reflex decision matches configured matrix across pairwise combinations

Title: Lab worklist filters by status, priority and specimen type
Test_Steps: 1. Open lab worklist | 2. Apply filters for status, priority and specimen type | 3. Validate results
Expected_Result: Worklist shows only matching orders; filter combination applied with AND semantics

Title: STAT orders sort above routine in worklist
Preconditions: Mixed STAT and Routine orders pending
Test_Steps: 1. Open worklist with default sort | 2. Inspect order sequence
Expected_Result: STAT orders are prioritized above routine by default sort

Title: Create lab order via FHIR ServiceRequest
Preconditions: FHIR R4 endpoint enabled; OAuth token valid
Test_Steps: 1. POST ServiceRequest for a lab test | 2. GET created resource | 3. Validate fields
Expected_Result: ServiceRequest created with intent=order, status=active, subject and code; readable back via GET

Title: DiagnosticReport groups Observations for a panel
Test_Steps: 1. GET DiagnosticReport for the panel | 2. Inspect result references
Expected_Result: DiagnosticReport.result references all member Observations; status=final; code present

Title: Observation interpretation code reflects abnormal flag
Test_Steps: 1. GET Observation for abnormal result | 2. Inspect interpretation
Expected_Result: Observation.interpretation uses HL7 v3 code (e.g. H) consistent with the lab abnormal flag

Title: Reject malformed ServiceRequest with invalid code system
Test_Steps: 1. POST ServiceRequest with unknown code system URI | 2. Inspect response
Expected_Result: Server returns 4xx with OperationOutcome describing the invalid/unknown coding; no resource persisted

Title: Inbound ORU^R01 result message updates the order result
Preconditions: HL7 interface engine configured; matching order exists
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU^R01 with OBX result segments | 2. Inspect order/result in app
Expected_Result: Result from OBX is posted to the matching order; values, units and abnormal flags mapped correctly

Title: ORU with OBR-25 status F finalizes; P stays preliminary
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU with OBR-25=P | 2. Send corrected ORU with OBR-25=F | 3. Inspect states
Expected_Result: P maps to PRELIMINARY; F maps to FINAL; status transition applied correctly

Title: ORU correction (OBX-11=C) amends prior result
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU with OBX-11=C and new value | 2. Inspect result history
Expected_Result: Result amended; prior value retained in history; correction auditable

Title: Unmatched ORU with unknown placer order is quarantined
Test_Steps: 1. Send ORU referencing nonexistent placer order number | 2. Inspect handling
Expected_Result: Message is rejected/quarantined to an error queue with NAK; no orphan result attached to wrong patient

Title: ACK is returned for accepted ORU message
Test_Steps: 1. Send valid ORU^R01 | 2. Capture ACK
Expected_Result: Application ACK (MSA-1=AA) returned referencing the message control id

Title: Lab result access restricted to authorized roles
Preconditions: Patient lab results exist; restricted role configured
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as user without lab-view privilege | 2. Attempt to open patient lab results
Expected_Result: Access denied; results not displayed; unauthorized attempt is logged

Title: Lab result API enforces patient-scope authorization
Preconditions: Two patients with lab data; scoped token
Test_Steps: 1. Authenticate with token scoped to patient A | 2. Request Observation for patient B
Expected_Result: Cross-patient access denied (403/empty); no PHI leakage across patient boundary

Title: Result entry endpoint rejects injection payloads
Test_Steps: 1. Submit result value containing SQL/script payload | 2. Inspect storage and rendering
Expected_Result: Payload is sanitized/parameterized; no injection executed; value stored as inert text or rejected

Title: Every result state transition is captured in audit log
Test_Steps: 1. Enter, verify, then amend a result | 2. Open audit log for the result
Expected_Result: Audit log records each transition with user, datetime, prior and new state, and reason for amendment

Title: Audit log entries are immutable to lab users
Test_Steps: 1. Log in as lab user | 2. Attempt to edit or delete an audit entry
Expected_Result: Audit entries cannot be modified or deleted by application users; attempt denied and itself logged

Title: Result-to-order referential integrity enforced
Test_Steps: 1. Inspect schema constraints between result and order | 2. Attempt orphan result insert
Expected_Result: Foreign-key constraint prevents orphan results; integrity enforced at DB level

Title: Concurrent verification of same result is serialized
Test_Steps: 1. Open same result in two sessions | 2. Verify in both nearly simultaneously
Expected_Result: Optimistic/pessimistic locking prevents conflicting writes; one succeeds, other gets stale-data error

Title: Result entry grid is fully keyboard navigable
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate result grid using Tab/Arrow keys only | 2. Enter and save a value without mouse
Expected_Result: All fields reachable and operable via keyboard; logical focus order; visible focus indicator

Title: Critical/abnormal flags are not conveyed by color alone
Preconditions: Results with abnormal and critical flags shown
Test_Steps: 1. View result list with flags | 2. Inspect non-color cues
Expected_Result: Abnormal/critical status conveyed via text/icon/label in addition to color per WCAG 1.4.1

Title: Screen reader announces result value, unit and flag
Preconditions: Screen reader active on results view
Test_Steps: 1. Navigate to a flagged result with screen reader | 2. Listen to announcement
Expected_Result: Screen reader announces analyte name, value, unit and abnormal interpretation

Title: Explore result entry under rapid concept search and switching
Preconditions: Result entry available with many analytes
Test_Steps: 1. Rapidly search/switch analytes | 2. Enter partial values | 3. Navigate away and back
Expected_Result: No data loss, mis-binding of values to wrong analyte, or UI freeze; unsaved-change prompts behave

Title: Explore specimen lifecycle with interrupted/back navigation
Test_Steps: 1. Start collection, navigate back mid-flow | 2. Resume | 3. Reject then attempt result
Expected_Result: State remains consistent across interruptions; no inconsistent states (e.g. result on rejected specimen)

Title: Full order lifecycle PENDING to FINAL transitions are valid only in order
Test_Steps: 1. Drive order through collection, accession, result, verify | 2. Attempt skipping a state
Expected_Result: Only valid transitions permitted; skipping states (e.g. ORDERED to VERIFIED) is blocked

Title: Cannot result on a discontinued order
Test_Steps: 1. Discontinue an order | 2. Attempt to enter result
Expected_Result: Result entry blocked for discontinued orders; clear message; no result persisted

Title: Result release rule by priority, criticality and verification
Test_Steps: 1. Combine STAT/Routine x Normal/Critical x Verified/Unverified | 2. Evaluate release
Expected_Result: Release decision matches matrix: unverified never released; critical requires callback; routine-normal-verified auto-releases

Title: Specimen volume below minimum draws a QNS rejection
Test_Steps: 1. Record specimen volume below minimum | 2. Attempt to accept
Expected_Result: Specimen flagged Quantity Not Sufficient and routed to rejection/recollection

Title: Pairwise order priority vs specimen type vs department routing
Test_Steps: 1. Vary priority, specimen type and target department | 2. Validate routing
Expected_Result: Each combination routes to the correct department worklist per rules; no misrouting

Title: Print/label specimen with accession barcode
Test_Steps: 1. Print specimen label | 2. Scan barcode | 3. Match to accession
Expected_Result: Label prints with scannable barcode encoding the accession; scan resolves to correct specimen/order

Title: Duplicate active order for same analyte is warned
Test_Steps: 1. Order the same analyte again within duplicate window | 2. Observe warning
Expected_Result: Duplicate-order warning shown with prior order details; clinician may override with reason

Title: Microbiology culture with staged preliminary then final results
Test_Steps: 1. Enter Gram stain preliminary | 2. Enter 24h growth preliminary | 3. Enter final identification and susceptibility
Expected_Result: Staged results recorded over time; each preliminary preserved; final supersedes without losing history

Title: Antimicrobial susceptibility result includes interpretation S/I/R
Test_Steps: 1. Enter MIC and S/I/R interpretation | 2. GET Observation | 3. Validate coding
Expected_Result: Susceptibility Observation carries organism, antibiotic and S/I/R interpretation with appropriate codes
